authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: O'Shaughnessy Dam (Hetch Hetchy Reservoir) to Clavey River Falls
La Grange Dam to Hickman Bridge
Hickman Bridge to the mouth
reaches.toDesc: mouth (confluence with the San Joaquin River)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(156) — Tuolumne River (Stanislaus and Tuolumne Cos.), O'Shaughnessy Dam to Clavey River Falls
sources.quotedText: (156) Tuolumne River (Stanislaus and Tuolumne Cos.) from O'Shaughnessy Dam (Hetch Hetchy Reservoir) downstream to Clavey River Falls. All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(115) — Tuolumne River (Stanislaus and Tuolumne Cos.), La Grange Dam to the mouth
sources.quotedText: (115) Tuolumne River (Stanislaus and Tuolumne Cos.). (A) From La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge. Jan. 1 through Oct. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession. (B) From Hickman Bridge to the mouth. Jan. 1 through Oct. 31. Bait may be used from Jan. 1 through Oct. 31. However, from Apr. 1 through the Fri. preceding the fourth Sat. in May, bait may be used only with single hooks having a gap between 1/2 and 1 inch, or with multiple hooks having a gap between 1/4 and 1/2 inch. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — hatchery trout/steelhead footnote (adipose fin clip requirement)
sources.quotedText: ** Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: Field Note: Floating the Wild and Scenic Tuolumne River — CalTrout
The Lower Tuolumne — California Fly Fisher
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: This special regulation applies to the Tuolumne River from O'Shaughnessy Dam (Hetch Hetchy Reservoir) downstream to Clavey River Falls.

regulations.humanSummary: Special regulation applies from O'Shaughnessy Dam (Hetch Hetchy Reservoir) downstream to Clavey River Falls
regulations.verbatimText: Tuolumne River (Stanislaus and Tuolumne Cos.) from O'Shaughnessy Dam (Hetch Hetchy Reservoir) downstream to Clavey River Falls.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year from O'Shaughnessy Dam to Clavey River Falls

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used; live or dead bait is prohibited.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures only, no bait, from O'Shaughnessy Dam to Clavey River Falls
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit; possession limit equals one daily bag limit (2) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a) absent a separately stated possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag and possession limit from O'Shaughnessy Dam to Clavey River Falls

parameters.note: Closed to fishing November 1 through December 31 from La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge.

regulations.humanSummary: Open January 1 through October 31 from La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge

parameters.note: Applies from La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 in possession, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag, 4 in possession, from La Grange Dam downstream to Hickman Bridge
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Closed to fishing November 1 through December 31 from Hickman Bridge to the mouth.

regulations.humanSummary: Open January 1 through October 31 from Hickman Bridge to the mouth

parameters.note: Bait may be used from Jan. 1 through Oct. 31. From Apr. 1 through the Friday preceding the fourth Saturday in May, bait may be used only with single hooks having a gap between 1/2 and 1 inch, or with multiple hooks having a gap between 1/4 and 1/2 inch.

regulations.humanSummary: Bait allowed Jan. 1–Oct. 31 from Hickman Bridge to the mouth; Apr. 1 through the Friday before the fourth Saturday in May, bait restricted to specific hook-gap sizes
regulations.verbatimText: Bait may be used from Jan. 1 through Oct. 31. However, from Apr. 1 through the Fri. preceding the fourth Sat. in May, bait may be used only with single hooks having a gap between 1/2 and 1 inch, or with multiple hooks having a gap between 1/4 and 1/2 inch.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 in possession, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag, 4 in possession, from Hickman Bridge to the mouth
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only trout/steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained as "hatchery" fish; wild fish with the adipose fin intact must be released immediately and do not count toward the bag or possession limit. Applies to the lower river below La Grange Dam.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (adipose-fin-intact) steelhead and trout must be released unharmed; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish may be kept
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
